fixes: Background article backfill now skips oversized pages before they enter WebKit, reducing the chance that one huge HTML page inflates Freed Desktop memory
Freed Desktop now rejects oversized cached reader HTML before loading it into WebKit and prunes legacy oversized cache files at startup
Startup content-signal backfill now waits until after launch, runs through the background runtime gate, and continues in smaller paced batches
Freed Desktop now builds the feed item UI projection without deep-cloning full article text first, reducing startup allocation spikes on large libraries
Loaded libraries no longer rebuild Automerge history just to check for possible savings when no synced text changed, avoiding another startup allocation spike
Clean startup loads now hydrate the first UI state without reserializing or rewriting an unchanged Automerge document
followUps: Project desktop feed items without full clone
Keeps explicit reader loads available while the lower-priority background path stays under a native response-size budget
Shared content-signal backfill now scans feed items in one pass instead of building stale item arrays for every batch

releaseBody:
(AI Generated).

## Freed v26.5.3004-dev

Lower startup memory pressure

### Fixes

- Background article backfill now skips oversized pages before they enter WebKit, reducing the chance that one huge HTML page inflates Freed Desktop memory
- Freed Desktop now rejects oversized cached reader HTML before loading it into WebKit and prunes legacy oversized cache files at startup
- Startup content-signal backfill now waits until after launch, runs through the background runtime gate, and continues in smaller paced batches
- Freed Desktop now builds the feed item UI projection without deep-cloning full article text first, reducing startup allocation spikes on large libraries
- Loaded libraries no longer rebuild Automerge history just to check for possible savings when no synced text changed, avoiding another startup allocation spike
- Clean startup loads now hydrate the first UI state without reserializing or rewriting an unchanged Automerge document

### Follow-ups

- Skip unchanged Automerge history probes
- Project desktop feed items without full clone
- Cap background article fetch size
- Safer overnight article backfill
- Keeps explicit reader loads available while the lower-priority background path stays under a native response-size budget
- Shared content-signal backfill now scans feed items in one pass instead of building stale item arrays for every batch

### Downloads

**macOS:** `.dmg` (Apple Silicon or Intel, signed + notarized)  
**Windows:** `.exe` (NSIS installer)  
**Linux:** `.AppImage`  

> macOS downloads are signed and notarized for normal Gatekeeper installation.
